findings.description: Package-level variable uses snake_case (item_cache): Go convention requires camelCase for unexported identifiers
Struct name inventory_item uses snake_case: Go convention requires CamelCase for type names (InventoryItem if exported, inventoryItem if unexported)
Mixed field naming within struct: item_count and is_active use snake_case while ItemName and CategoryID use CamelCase, violating consistent naming within the type
Exported function GetItem lacks a doc comment: Go convention requires comments on all exported symbols (golint/revive)
Exported-style function naming inconsistency: update_item_count is unexported but uses snake_case instead of camelCase (updateItemCount)

notes: All findings are style violations — no correctness or security issues. The ClearExpired function at line 40 also lacks a doc comment and is not guarded by the mutex, but the mutex omission would be a correctness finding outside the scope of this checker-focused fixture.
